copy.caption: The silent snowshoer who never leaves a print you'd notice.
copy.shortDescription: Lynxes have chunky, oversized paws that spread wide and act like built-in snowshoes, letting them walk right across deep snow that a deer would sink into. Their short spotted tail has a black tip, and their cheek fur puffs out like a full beard in winter. Fun fact: A Canada lynx and the snowshoe hare it hunts go through population booms and crashes together — when hares crash, lynx numbers crash shortly after.

photo.attributionName: Jan Czeczotka (c)"Programa de Conservación Ex-situ del Lince Ibérico www.lynxexsitu.es" Denali National Park and Preserve Becker1999 (Paul and Cathy)
